scenario: Clean On-Label (AUTO-APPROVE) — verified HCP asks a straightforward Contraindications question about CholoClear-X. All gates pass -> auto-commit. Adds an auto-approve case (different PI section) so auto-approve PRECISION isn't measured on a single example.
input_text:
SOURCE CHANNEL: Web Portal
SUBMISSION PORTAL REF: portal-inbound-auth-5521

MESSAGE BODY:
CardioMed Medical Information,

I am a clinical pharmacist reviewing a new start for CholoClear-X. Could you confirm whether CholoClear-X is contraindicated in patients with active hepatic disease or persistently elevated serum transaminases? I want to verify against the approved label before we dispense.

Thank you,
Priya Nair, PharmD
Cleveland Clinic
Email: pnair@clevelandclinic.org | Phone: 216-555-0140

on_label_record.inquiry_summary_text: Pharmacist asking whether CholoClear-X is contraindicated in patients with active hepatic disease or elevated transaminases.

labeling_notes: AUTO-APPROVE: a clean, in-scope, on-label label question from a registry-verified HCP. No safety markers, product matches catalog (PROD_CHOLO_MED), reporter resolves to NET_HCP_61205 -> all gates pass -> record auto-commits as Approved.

DISTINCT FROM case_001/023 (Dosage and Administration): this targets the Contraindications section -> suggested_srd_match SRD_CHOLO_CONTRA_v1. Two clean auto-approves (this + case_030) let auto-approve PRECISION be measured on more than one example.
NO PILLARS: on-label info request, not a safety event -> the 4 ICSR pillars default False. Native-digital -> Gate 1 passes via the no-OCR path.
